commit.message:
fix(tests): flaky Chat Client E2E tests and failing CreateServer unit test (#8887)

* Initial plan

* Fix flaky and failing Chat Client E2E tests

- Fix 5 flaky tests (strict mode violations) in username-display.spec.ts and
  message-variations.spec.ts by adding .first() to getByText() locators.
  The SSE mock echo response 'I received your message: ...' contains the
  sent text, causing Playwright to find 2 elements and fail strict mode.
- Fix consistently failing test 'should disable input while waiting for
  response' in input-validation.spec.ts by adding a test-specific route
  handler for message/stream that delays the response by 3 seconds,
  ensuring the agent request stays in-flight long enough to observe the
  disabled input state (previously route.continue() failed immediately
  with no real backend, causing the input to re-enable too quickly).
- Extract delay as named constant MESSAGE_STREAM_MOCK_DELAY_MS for clarity.

Co-authored-by: ccastrotrejo <102700317+ccastrotrejo@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix unit test: CreateServer closes panel after successful submission

Two related fixes:

1. libs/designer/src/lib/ui/mcp/panel/server/create.tsx:
   Move dispatch(closePanel()) inside the try block (after await onUpdate())
   instead of after the finally block. This is semantically correct (panel
   should only close on success, not when onUpdate throws) and avoids subtle
   Promise chain timing issues in async tests with React 18 + jsdom.

2. libs/designer/src/lib/ui/mcp/panel/server/__test__/create.spec.tsx:
   Rewrite "closes panel after successful submission" test to use
   fireEvent.change instead of userEvent.type. Fluent UI's tabster
   focus-trapping library intercepts keyboard events fired by userEvent.type,
   causing only partial text to be typed (CI evidence: value="T" in HTML
   snapshot instead of value="TestServer"). Use waitFor to wait for the
   submit button to become enabled before clicking, matching the established
   pattern in the file (test "calls onUpdate with correct data" at line 472).

Co-authored-by: ccastrotrejo <102700317+ccastrotrejo@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Initial plan

Co-authored-by: crramirez <8397379+crramirez@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: crramirez <8397379+crramirez@users.noreply.github.com>

* Optimize ImageRGB: inline alpha-blend math, remove unnecessary parallel streams

- alphaBlendOver: Replace per-pixel ImageUtils.blendColors() call with
  inlined integer arithmetic (multiply + shift instead of double math),
  precompute (1-alpha), eliminate 2 Rgb record allocations per pixel
- getSubimage: Remove parallel streams from System.arraycopy loop
  (memory-bandwidth-bound, parallelization adds overhead)
- fillRect: Remove parallel streams from Arrays.fill loop
  (JVM intrinsic saturates memory bus on single core)
- resizeCanvas: Remove parallel streams, split into two sequential
  loops for better branch prediction

* Add explicit parentheses in alphaBlendOver for readability

commit.message:
Add JsonPatch.ToBinaryData() for efficient binary output (#56791)

* Add JsonPatch.ToBinaryData() for efficient binary output

Adds ToBinaryData() and ToBinaryData(string format) methods to JsonPatch,
enabling efficient retrieval of UTF-8 binary data without the unnecessary
string roundtrip required by ToString() + BinaryData.FromString().

This addresses the inefficiency in the workaround suggested at
https://github.com/openai/openai-dotnet/issues/733#issuecomment-4001463519
where users had to do BinaryData.FromString(patch.ToString("J")) to feed
patched JSON into ModelReaderWriter.Read. Now they can simply use
patch.ToBinaryData("J") which avoids the UTF-8 -> string -> UTF-8 roundtrip.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add ToBinaryData vs ToString benchmarks

Adds BenchmarkDotNet benchmarks comparing ToBinaryData("J") against
ToString("J") + BinaryData.FromString() for seeded and unseeded scenarios.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Remove Web SDK — plain .NET SDK with BmwConfig (fixes #925)

Replace Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web with Microsoft.NET.Sdk + FrameworkReference.
Eliminate Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration entirely.

BmwConfig — pipe-delimited bootstrap config (Metal.config):
  Key|value format, one per line, # comments. Read at startup via
  BmwConfig.Load(directory). Provides GetValue<T>, GetArray, HasKey.
  Only used for pre-data-store bootstrap (data root, log folder, listen
  address, Kestrel tuning). Runtime config continues through SettingsService.

Program.cs — no WebApplication, no WebApplicationBuilder:
  Replaced ConfigurationBuilder chain with BmwConfig.Load(contentRoot).
  Replaced app.Run() with BmwHost.Create(server, configureKestrel) +
  host.RunAsync(). The app is now a plain .NET console process that
  happens to host a Kestrel server.

ProgramSetup — all methods take BmwConfig instead of WebApplication/IConfiguration:
  ConfigureKestrel returns Action<KestrelServerOptions> (no builder dependency).
  CreateLogger, CreateDataStore, CreateClientRequestTracker, CreateAppInfo,
  ConfigureStaticFiles, ConfigureCors, ConfigureHttps, ConfigureProxyRoutes
  all take BmwConfig + string contentRoot.

BareMetalWebServer — replaced IConfiguration with BmwConfig:
  Constructor takes BmwConfig + contentRootPath. IBareWebHost interface updated.

RouteHandlers — eliminated context.RequestServices.GetService(IConfiguration):
  Three methods (GetEntraIdOptions, GetUploadRootPath, GetLogRoot) now read
  from BmwConfig passed at construction. No more service locator pattern.

csproj changes:
  - Sdk: Microsoft.NET.Sdk (was Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web)
  - Added: OutputType Exe, FrameworkReference Microsoft.AspNetCore.App
  - Content: Metal.config (replaces appsettings.json)

What BMW still gets from the shared framework (via FrameworkReference):
  HttpContext, Kestrel server, HTTP/1.1+2+3 parsers, TLS, socket
  transport, pipelines, zero-copy streaming — the good parts, without
  the middleware/routing/DI infrastructure.

Co-authored-by: root <root@localhost.localdomain>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

why
currently vpp doesn't support binding multiple ACL tables. Each table is appended with default permit-all rules. With multiple tables, this may cause acl matched by such rules and skip the actual rule to make in the tables after this one.

what this PR does
remove the default permit-all rules for each table
If a table is empty, create a dummy rule that won't match any traffic because vpp doesn't allow empty table. The dummy rule matches dest-ip to 0.0.0.0/32
sort all the tables by priority in the table group. vpp doesn't support parallel matching
added catch-all acl group to the end. vpp default behavior of no match is drop but sonic is accept.
Fix sonic-vpp crashing due to race condition during stats pull. If the interface to get stats has been removed, stat_segment_ls_r returns null.

test(w69): fun + walk + content deep tests (~60 tests) (#587)

## Summary

Add deep integration tests for three feature-gated crates covering ~62
tests total.

### tokmd-fun (20 tests)
- OBJ rendering: determinism, structural invariants (vertex/face counts
scale linearly), face index validity, quad faces, name sanitization,
input order preservation
- Edge cases: zero-height buildings, all-zero dimensions, negative
coordinates, single-building eco-label proxy
- MIDI generation: determinism, empty notes, zero tempo clamping,
channel clamping, simultaneous notes, overlapping notes, high tempo

### tokmd-walk (20 tests)
- File discovery: flat dirs, nested dirs, relative paths, empty dirs
- Max-file limits: zero returns empty, max-one, exceeds file count
- Deterministic ordering across calls
- Hidden file handling (dotfiles)
- License/metadata candidate detection: standard licenses, manifests,
case-insensitive, sorting, determinism
- file_size: exact bytes, empty files, missing file errors, determinism

### tokmd-content (22 tests)
- Entropy: empty, uniform, 2-value (~1 bit), max (~8 bits), determinism
- Tag counting: basic detection, multiple occurrences, case-insensitive,
empty edge cases
- BLAKE3 hashing: determinism, hex format, collision resistance
- Text detection: ASCII, UTF-8, null bytes (binary), empty
- File reading: read_head limits, read_head_tail, read_lines zero max,
hash_file consistency
- Complexity: analyze_functions (empty, Rust, Python), cyclomatic
(empty, branching), cognitive, nesting depth

All tests are deterministic and pass on the current main branch.
